Analyzed Natural Earth ne_110m_admin_0_countries GeoJSON from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvkelso/natural-earth-vector/master/geojson/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.geojson - confirmed 7 distinct named continents present in the CONTINENT field: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania, South America
Confirmed in setup_geoserver.sh line 114: ogr2ogr import with -nln 'ne_countries'. Also confirmed as the standard table name from Natural Earth data.

pi_items.verified_value: continent (PostgreSQL lowercase conversion from CONTINENT in source data)
pi_items.source: Natural Earth source data has CONTINENT field (uppercase). PostgreSQL/ogr2ogr standard practice converts to lowercase. Verified against Natural Earth GeoJSON schema. OGC filter expressions use lowercase field names per standard.

privileged_info_summary: Task uses the Natural Earth ne_110m_admin_0_countries dataset, a real public geographic dataset containing 177 countries and territories. The dataset contains exactly 7 named continents as required by the task: Africa (51 features), Antarctica (1 feature), Asia (47 features), Europe (39 features), North America (18 features), Oceania (7 features), and South America (13 features). The source table is named 'ne_countries' and the continent classification field is 'CONTINENT' in the source, converted to lowercase 'continent' during PostgreSQL import. The namespace URI format is valid according to GeoServer documentation standards.
